text.title: Synthetic Users Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the synthetic-users methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Every output row MUST carry `synthetic: true` and a `model_id` field; never present mixed real+synthetic data without per-row flags.
rule.rationale: Stripping the label is the single largest cause of stakeholders mistaking AI guesses for survey data.

rule.statement: Synthetic output MUST cite a planned real-user validation step (date, sample size) before any product decision references it.
rule.rationale: Synthetic-only decisions have a documented failure rate that destroys methodology trust.

rule.statement: Synthetic responses MUST NOT be used to set price points, demand forecasts, or revenue commitments.
rule.rationale: LLM personas systematically overstate willingness-to-pay and downstream financial estimates.

rule.statement: Each persona MUST cite ≥3 evidence anchors (real interview snippet, segment data point, ethnography note); abstract personas are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Ungrounded personas converge on a generic stereotype and produce no useful variance.

rule.statement: At least 30% of generated responses MUST be flagged adversarial (edge-case, hostile, off-script); pure 'happy path' panels are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without adversarial samples synthetic panels confirm the prompt and surface no risk.

rule.statement: Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
